investigations: CBC with differential (CRITICAL — assess for leukopenia from methotrexate)
imaging: Chest X-ray (screen for pneumonia, pulmonary pathology)
CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast if clinical concern for complicated UTI or abscess
medications: IV fluid bolus 500 mL normal saline (assess response — repeat if needed)
Empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics: piperacillin-tazobactam 4.5 g IV q8h (covers ESBL — do NOT use TMP-SMX due to likely resistance and methotrexate interaction)
Hold methotrexate pending CBC result and infectious workup
Continue metoprolol 50 mg BID (do not abruptly discontinue beta-blocker)
Continue metformin unless creatinine elevated or contrast planned
Paracetamol 1 g for any discomfort (avoid NSAIDs)
contraindications_flagged: Avoid NSAIDs — documented allergy (GI bleeding) and renal risk in immunosuppressed diabetic patient
Avoid nephrotoxic agents — immunosuppressed + diabetic with potential renal impairment
Caution with aminoglycosides — renal toxicity risk; use only if no alternative for resistant organism
Avoid TMP-SMX — likely resistant organism AND dangerous interaction with methotrexate (folate antagonism potentiation)
reasoning: 79-year-old immunosuppressed female (methotrexate + prednisone) presenting with generalized weakness 6 days after UTI treatment. While vital signs are normal, the combination of immunosuppression, recent UTI with resistant organism, and vague symptoms in an elderly patient warrants a thorough infectious workup. CBC is the single most critical lab — leukopenia from methotrexate (especially given concurrent TMP-SMX folate antagonism) would significantly change management. Blood cultures and lactate should be obtained before starting empiric antibiotics. Piperacillin-tazobactam provides ESBL coverage and avoids the problematic TMP-SMX + methotrexate interaction. Methotrexate should be held pending results. Admission is warranted for monitoring, IV antibiotics, and serial clinical reassessment.
